title: Quiz: Federated Quantum Classifiers
description: Test your understanding of federated quantum classification

questions.question: What is the main benefit of federated quantum classifiers?

options.text: Faster training on a single device
Training across organizations without sharing raw data
Eliminating the need for quantum hardware
Perfect classification accuracy

questions.explanation: Federated learning allows collaborative training while keeping each participant's data private and local.

questions.question: What is 'client drift' in federated learning?

options.text: Clients physically moving locations
Local models diverging from the global optimum due to non-IID data
Network connection instability
Clients leaving the federation

questions.explanation: Client drift occurs when local training on heterogeneous data causes models to diverge from what would be optimal globally.

questions.question: What does FedProx add to the local training objective?

options.text: A term that speeds up training
A regularizer penalizing deviation from the global model
A term that increases privacy
A term that reduces communication

questions.explanation: FedProx adds μ/2 ||θ_local - θ_global||² to keep local updates close to the global model.

questions.question: In personalized federated learning, all parameters are shared across clients.

options.text: True
False

questions.explanation: False. Personalization typically splits parameters into shared (federated) and personal (local-only) components.

questions.question: What is 'label skew' in non-IID data?

options.text: All clients have the same labels
Different clients have different class distributions
Labels are incorrectly assigned
Labels are encrypted

questions.explanation: Label skew means clients have different proportions of classes, e.g., one hospital might see more of certain conditions.

questions.question: Why is worst-client accuracy an important metric?

options.text: It's the only metric that matters
It ensures the model is fair and useful for all participants
It's always equal to average accuracy
It measures communication efficiency

questions.explanation: Worst-client accuracy ensures no participant is left behind with a poorly performing model.

questions.question: In the federated training loop, what happens after local training?

options.text: Models are deleted
Client updates are aggregated into a new global model
Training stops
Raw data is sent to the server

questions.explanation: After local training, client model updates are aggregated (e.g., using FedAvg) to produce a new global model.

questions.question: Which are valid personalization strategies? (Select all)

options.text: Sharing base layers, keeping top layers personal
Fine-tuning global model on local data
Sending all raw data to the server
Using separate personal heads for each client

questions.explanation: Personalization involves adapting global models locally through layer splitting, fine-tuning, or personal heads—not sending raw data.
